claims.evidence_text: the gross irrigation requirement of MBD is 59.8 and net irrigation is 41.9 mm less than CWR which is 180 mm
claims.claim_summary: Total seasonal crop water requirement (evapotranspiration) of wheat is about 180 mm in the Mandibahauddin zone of Punjab.

claims.evidence_text: The total irrigation water requirement of wheat in MBD, FSD, TTS, BWN, BWP, and KNP is 44.7, 102.2, 103.1, 119.1, 179, and 184.2 mm, respectively.
claims.claim_summary: Seasonal net irrigation water requirement of wheat across six Punjab agro-climatic zones ranged from 44.7 to 184.2 mm.

claims.evidence_text: the lowest water requirement is in the initial stage, and the highest water requirement is in the mid-stage during crop development... This study shows that the wheat crop requires more water during the growing stage than the other three stages.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat water demand is lowest at the initial stage and highest at the mid (development/growing) stage of the crop cycle.

claims.evidence_text: The soil type of the study area ranges from sandy loam to loam. ... Punjab soil is favorable for wheat production
claims.claim_summary: Wheat in the Punjab study zones is grown on soils ranging from sandy loam to loam, considered favorable for wheat.
